network.overall_strategy: Integration of cyanosulfidic Strecker synthesis, hydrothermal reductive amination, and meteorite-supported hydantoin pathways using mineral catalysts and UV photochemistry to address threonine's synthesis-stability paradox

reactions.name: UV-driven acetaldehyde cyanohydrin formation
reactions.mechanism: Photoredox cycling converts HCN to lactaldehyde via cyanohydrin intermediate
reactions.reasoning: Ritson & Sutherland (2013) demonstrated 19% lactaldehyde yield from acetaldehyde cyanohydrin under UV with H₂S reductant

reactions.name: Strecker synthesis of threonine aminonitrile
reactions.mechanism: Lactaldehyde reacts with NH₃ and HCN to form aminonitrile via imine intermediate
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) cyanosulfidic framework produces threonine aminonitrile as inevitable by-product

reactions.name: Hydrothermal reductive amination
reactions.mechanism: Fe/Ni-catalyzed CO-dependent reductive amination of α-keto-β-hydroxybutyrate
reactions.reasoning: Huber & Wächtershäuser (2006) demonstrated analogous α-amino acid synthesis on Ni-Fe precipitates

reactions.name: Bucherer-Bergs hydantoin formation
reactions.reasoning: Pulletikurti et al. (2022) demonstrated hydantoin pathway for amino acid synthesis

reactions.name: Hydantoin hydrolysis to threonine
reactions.mechanism: Hydrolytic ring-opening of hydantoin to amino acid
reactions.reasoning: Lerner & Cooper (2005) detected hydantoins in meteorites with hydrolysis potential

reactions.name: Spark discharge synthesis
reactions.mechanism: Plasma-driven formation of HCN and aldehydes
reactions.reasoning: Miller et al. (1972) detected threonine in spark discharge experiments with H₂S

reactions.name: Formose reaction initiation
reactions.mechanism: HCN oligomerization to glycolaldehyde via formose-like chemistry
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) cyanosulfidic network produces sugar precursors

reactions.name: Aminonitrile hydrolysis
reactions.mechanism: Hydrolytic conversion of aminonitrile to amino acid
reactions.reasoning: Standard Strecker synthesis completion as per Ritson & Sutherland (2013)

pathways.name: CyanoSulfidic Strecker
pathways.description: UV-driven HCN chemistry through lactaldehyde to threonine aminonitrile

pathways.name: Hydrothermal Reductive Amination
pathways.description: Fe/Ni-catalyzed synthesis from α-keto acid precursor

pathway_evaluations.feedback: CyanoSulfidic Strecker pathway aligns strongly with Ritson-Sutherland experiments. UV-driven acetaldehyde cyanohydrin formation (rxn_001) and Strecker synthesis (rxn_002) are well-supported. Hydrolysis (rxn_008) is chemically valid. Missing explicit mineral catalysts (e.g., Cu) in reaction descriptions lowers environmental progression score.
suggestions: Specify Cu(I) cyanide catalysts for rxn_001/rxn_002 as per Patel et al. (2015)
Clarify environmental transitions (e.g., UV-exposed surface pools)

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Hydrothermal reductive amination (rxn_003) lacks a defined route to α-keto-β-hydroxybutyrate (mol_007). While Fe²⁺/Ni catalysis (Kaur et al., 2024) is plausible, the absence of keto acid formation steps creates a critical gap. Environmental justification for hydrothermal → biochemical transition is weak.
suggestions: Add keto acid synthesis step (e.g., CO₂ fixation or oxidation of lactaldehyde)
Specify Ni/Fe-S mineral catalysts and H₂ conditions

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Bucherer-Bergs pathway (rxn_004 → rxn_005) aligns with Pulletikurti et al. (2022). Hydantoin hydrolysis (rxn_005) is valid. Surface-to-biochemical transition is logical. Missing ammonium carbonate in hydantoin formation (rxn_004) lowers feasibility.
suggestions: Include NH₃/CO₂ in rxn_004 reactants for authentic Bucherer-Bergs conditions

evaluation.network_feedback: Network integrates key literature-supported mechanisms (Strecker, Bucherer-Bergs) but has critical gaps: (1) Missing α-keto-β-hydroxybutyrate synthesis undermines P2; (2) Mineral catalysts (Cu, Fe-S, Ni) under-specified; (3) Environmental transitions need explicit justification. Hub molecules (lactaldehyde, aminonitrile) are well-connected. Approved due to P1/P3 viability.
